module.title: SAP TM Reporting, Analytics & Performance Management

learningObjectives.title: Understand exception management
learningObjectives.description: Explain detection, alerting, and correction of transportation deviations.

learningObjectives.title: Understand operational analytics
learningObjectives.description: Describe real-time exception visibility and KPI-driven operational control.

learningObjectives.title: Understand response effectiveness
learningObjectives.description: Clarify how resolution metrics improve service reliability and recovery rates.

sections.content: Exception Management in SAP Transportation Management identifies, monitors and resolves transportation issues that deviate from planned execution. Operational Analytics provides real-time visibility into these exceptions through dashboards, alerts and KPIs, enabling transportation planners to take corrective actions before service levels are affected.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Operational Analytics tells you what is happening now, while Exception Management highlights what requires immediate attention.

A truck transporting medical supplies experiences an unexpected breakdown. GPS tracking immediately reports the event to SAP TM. An exception alert appears on the planner's dashboard, who assigns another vehicle to complete the delivery and minimize customer impact.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Configure automatic alerts for critical transportation exceptions such as shipment delays, route deviations and temperature violations so planners can respond immediately.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A pharmaceutical company integrated SAP TM with GPS and IoT monitoring. Real-time exception alerts reduced cold-chain delivery failures by over 35% and significantly improved regulatory compliance.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Remember: Monitoring observes all transportation activities, whereas Exception Management focuses only on events that require corrective action.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Not every transportation event is an exception. Only deviations from planned execution that require business intervention should trigger exception management.

items: Exception Management detects transportation issues requiring action.
Alerts help planners resolve issues quickly.
KPIs measure exception frequency and resolution performance.
Effective exception management improves transportation reliability and customer service.
